envelope_assembly: Project-specific; durable waterfront/institutional envelope where applicable

sources.title: Time-Saver Standards for Building Types — Marinas, Swimming Docks, Camps and Nature Center sections
USACE shoreline management planning documents (used only for public-waterfront operational context, not dimensional standards)
ADA Standards for Accessible Design (for gangways, routes, toilets, and public access)

small.name: Typical Municipal Marina
small.notes: Harbormaster office; restrooms; limited retail; transient slips

medium.name: Harbor East Marina
medium.notes: Marina office, showers, docks, waterfront retail/restaurant edge

space_library.name: Lobby / Harbormaster Reception
notes: Public-facing check-in, permits, and slip assignment.
Keep direct visual connection to docks where feasible.

space_library.name: Harbormaster Office / Operations
notes: Operational control, radio, records, and emergency coordination.

space_library.name: Dock Access Control / Gangway Landing
notes: Critical land-water threshold; dimensional design is project-specific and tide-dependent.
Use ADA-compliant accessible route where required.

space_library.name: Public Restrooms / Showers
notes: Common in transient marinas; showers may be controlled-access.

space_library.name: Laundry / Boater Support
notes: Optional for transient and liveaboard-support facilities.

space_library.name: Cafe / Club Lounge
notes: Optional mixed-use waterfront amenity; use only where operator model supports it.

space_library.name: Service Pantry / Kitchen
notes: Populate detailed kitchen data in later pass; grease and exhaust design deferred.

space_library.name: Service Yard / Marine Support Storage
notes: Boat-support gear, maintenance supplies, refuse, and dock cart staging.

space_library.name: Fuel Dock / Fueling Station
notes: Fuel dock requires fire-rated separation from occupied buildings per NFPA 30A and local fire marshal requirements.
Spill containment, emergency shutoff, and vapor recovery systems typically required.
